Label: behaviour change intervention
Description: An intervention that has the aim of influencing human behaviour.

Label: social alienation
Description: A mental disposition to perceive or experience oneself as isolated from and not meaningfully involved in social groups.

Label: psychological need
Description: Psychological needs can be satisfied or thwarted.

Label: location
Description: An intervention could be used to rearrange objects in an environment, changing the location of a person relative to these objects and thereby increasing the likelihood of a behaviour.

Label: behaviour change intervention scenario plan
Description: A plan that is realized in a BCI scenario process.

Label: population behaviour
Description: The class human behaviour [BCIO:042000] has been made obsolete. If you want to refer to the behaviour of an individual, refer to individual human behaviour . If you want to refer to the behaviour of a group, refer to population behaviour.

Label: individual human behaviour
Description: A bodily process of a human that involves co-ordinated contraction of striated muscles controlled by the brain.

Label: human life function
Description: There are complex sets of processes involved in the realisation of human life functions, only some which would behaviours. An example for a human life function that is not a behaviour function: human thermoregulation via sweating function. However, some behaviours can fulfil 'human life functions'.  The term 'survive' refers to continuing one's existence (e.g., staying alive), while 'thrive' includes a wide range of processes that improve an animal's life, including physical and mental wellbeing and experiencing positive social interactions, as well as being able to reproduce. Many processes can contribute to a human surviving and thriving.

Label: process aggregate
Description: An occurrent consisting exactly of a plurality of processes that are process_aggregate_member_parts_of that occurrent for all times at which it exists.

Label: intervention
Description: A planned process that has the aim of influencing an outcome.

Label: human behaviour
Description: The superclass of all human behaviour types in the BCIO; use individual human behaviour or population behaviour for specific cases.

Label: process attribute
Description: A process profile that is an attribute of a process.

Label: behavioural attribute
Description: A process attribute of an individual human behaviour.

Label: entity
Description: The root class of the Basic Formal Ontology hierarchy; everything that exists in reality.

Label: continuant
Description: An entity that persists, endures, or continues to exist through time while maintaining its identity.

Label: occurrent
Description: An entity that unfolds itself in time or has temporal parts, phases, or stages.

Label: independent continuant
Description: A continuant that is a bearer of quality and realizable entity entities, in which other entities inhere and which itself cannot inhere in anything.

Label: process
Description: An occurrent that has temporal proper parts and for some time t, p s-depends_on some material entity at t.

Label: disposition
Description: A realizable entity whose basis is the physical make-up of the bearer, which causes the bearer to behave in certain ways given certain triggers or circumstances.

Label: realizable entity
Description: A specifically dependent continuant that inheres in continuant entities and is not exhibited in full at every time it inheres in an entity.

Label: quality
Description: A specifically dependent continuant that, in contrast to roles and dispositions, does not require any further process in order to be realized.

Label: specifically dependent continuant
Description: A continuant that inheres in or is borne by other entities. Every instance of A requires some specific instance of B which must always be the same.

Label: generically dependent continuant
Description: A continuant that is dependent on one or other independent continuant bearers. For every instance of A requires some instance of (an independent continuant type) B but which instance of B serves can change from time to time.

Label: function
Description: A disposition that exists in virtue of the bearer's physical make-up and that came into being either through evolution or intentional design to realize processes of a certain sort.

Label: material entity
Description: An independent continuant that at all times at which it exists has some portion of matter as a proper or improper continuant part.

Label: process profile
Description: A process that is a part of a process such that there is some observable profile quality inhering in and realized by the process profile.

Label: biological_process
Description: Note that, in addition to forming the root of the biological process ontology, this term is recommended for the annotation of gene products whose biological process is unknown. When this term is used for annotation, it indicates that no information was available about the biological process of the gene product annotated as of the date the annotation was made; the evidence code 'no data' (ND), is used to indicate this.

Label: data entity
Description: An information content entity that is intended to be one or more truthful statements about something and is constructed or acquired by a method which reliably tends to produce approximately truthful statements.

Label: information content entity
Description: A generically dependent continuant that is about some thing.

Label: directive information entity
Description: An information content entity whose concretizations indicate to their bearer how to realize them in a process.

Label: curation status specification
Description: A data item that represents the curation status of an ontology term.

Label: data about an ontology part
Description: Data about an ontology part is a data item about a part of an ontology, for example a term, an axiom, or a property.

Label: obsolescence reason specification
Description: A specification of the reason why an ontology term was made obsolete.

Label: denotator type
Description: A specification for a type of denotation used to reference an entity.

Label: subjective affective feeling
Description: 'Subjective affective feeling can have greater or weaker physiological or mental components. The bodily process that are experienced will usually involve some mental process.  Bodily sensation and the experiences of emotions (e.g., feeling calm) would both qualify as examples of 'subjective affective feeling'.  As a subclass of 'affective process', subjective affective feelings have valence. Valence refers to the 'the subjective value of an event, object, person, or other entity in the life space of the individual' (https://dictionary.apa.org/valence). Valence ranges from negative to positive.

Label: affective process
Description: Valence refers to the 'the subjective value of an event, object, person, or other entity in the life space of the individual' (https://dictionary.apa.org/valence). Valence ranges from negative to positive.

Label: human being
Description: An extended organism that is a member of the species Homo sapiens.

Label: mental process
Description: A 'mental process' is a subclass of 'bodily process', as mental processes occur within the body, specifically involving the brain.

Label: bodily disposition
Description: Bodily disposition includes features that can be used in a specific context, such as a person's strength, but not a person's weight. A person has a specific weight independent of the circumstances they are in.

Label: mental disposition
Description: 'Mental disposition' is a subclass of 'bodily disposition', as mental dispositions occur in the body.

Label: bodily quality
Description: A quality that inheres in some extended organism.

Label: mental quality
Description: A quality that inheres in the mind; a mental characteristic or attribute of a person.

Label: planned process
Description: A process that realizes a plan which is the concretization of a plan specification.

Label: plan
Description: A realizable entity that inheres in a bearer who is committed to realizing it as a completely executed planned process.

Label: bodily process
Description: A process in which at least one bodily component of an organism participates.

Label: extended organism
Description: An object aggregate consisting of an organism and all material entities located within, overlapping, or occupying sites formed in part by the organism.

Label: reminiscence therapy process
Description: Reminiscence therapy is a psychosocial intervention for older adults, based on the concept that people review and reflect on significant personal past experiences to promote pleasure, better quality of life, and adaptability.

Label: cognitive appraisal representational pattern quality
Description: A cognitive representational pattern quality which consists of an evaluation of the relevance of some entity to another entity.

Label: self-appraisal representational pattern quality
Description: A cognitive appraisal representational pattern quality consisting of an evaluation of the appraiser themselves.

Label: self-efficacy representational pattern quality
Description: A self-appraisal representational pattern quality consisting of an evaluation of the agent's capability to achieve objectives or satisfy desires.

Label: psychological construct information content entity
Description: An information content entity that represents a theoretical construct used in psychology to describe, explain, or predict psychological phenomena.

Label: psychoanalytic psychological identity construct information content entity
Description: A psychological construct in which it is hypothesized that human consciousness consists of the id, ego, and superego.

Label: performative identity quality
Description: A personal identity quality formed as a result of the agent's performative behaviors as influencing and influenced by the formation of one's psychological identity.

Label: social withdrawal disposition
Description: A mental disposition that, if realized, is realized in the consistent avoidance of social interactions with both familiar and unfamiliar peers due to psychological traits such as social fear or anxiety.

Label: self-transcendence disposition
Description: A transcendence disposition that, if realized, is realized in shifting oneâs focus beyond the self toward broader existential, moral, or spiritual concerns, including care for others, connection with nature, or alignment with perceived universal values.

Label: elective solitude disposition
Description: A mental disposition that, if realized, is realized in intentionally withdrawing from social interaction in order to pursue self-reflection, restoration, or personal goals.

Label: imposed solitude characteristic
Description: A characteristic of a person that is physically or socially alone due to external constraints or circumstances, and not by personal choice.

Label: ambient sociability disposition
Description: A mental disposition that is realized in retaining the sense of social presence without direct social engagement with others in a public or semi-public space.

Label: healthy aging process
Description: A biological process that is realized in maintaining functional ability, emotional well-being, and social engagement in later life, relative to cultural and personal expectations.

Label: successful aging construct information content entity
Description: A psychological construct information content entity that describes achieving physical health, cognitive vitality, and continued involvement with life, as measured against individual and cultural standards of aging well.

Label: aloneliness disposition
Description: A mental disposition that, if realized, is realized in experiencing discomfort or distress due to a perceived lack of desired time alone.

Label: social detachment disposition
Description: A mental disposition that, if realized, is realized in reduced motivation to engage in or maintain social relationships, often without distress or social fear.

Label: shyness behavior disposition
Description: A mental disposition to social anxiety that, if realized, is realized in the inhibition of behavior during potential social interaction due to fear of negative evaluation.

Label: solitude affinity disposition
Description: A mental disposition that, if realized, is realized in seeking out and enjoying time spent alone for reflection, creativity, or restoration.

Label: representational pattern quality
Description: A quality that represents something information content entity, made up of different parts arranged in a certain way to form a whole, and is a characteristic of something, whether itâs physical or abstract.

Label: cognitive representational pattern quality
Description: A mental representation that represents an information content entity, made up of different parts arranged in a specific way to form a whole, and is a characteristic of something, physical or abstract, invovling skills involved in tasks related to perception, learning, memory, understanding, awareness, reasoning, judgment, intuition, and language.

Label: mental representational pattern quality
Description: A hypothetical entity representing a perception, thought, memory, or something similar during mental processes, made up of separate parts arranged in a way that forms a whole, and being a characteristic of something, whether physical or abstract.

Label: personal identity quality
Description: A quality that represents the stable and distinctive characteristics by which an individual is recognized and knows themselves over time.

Label: narrative identity quality
Description: A personal identity quality constituted by the internalized, evolving story an individual constructs to give their life a sense of unity and purpose across time.

Label: ego identity quality
Description: Ego identity may also be understood as an adaptive accomplishment or achievement of the individual vis-a-vis his social environment. Specifically, it is the adaptation of the individual's special skills, capacities, and strengths to the prevailing role structure of the society in which he lives.

Label: anxious solitude construct information content entity
Description: How are behavioral inhibition and anxious-solitude related to and distinct from solitude? (anxious-solitude, and behavioral inhibition are all constructs reflecting biologically-based dispositions to avoid others due to social fears and anxieties)

Label: social convention directive information entity
Description: Everyone conforms to R Everyone believes others conform to R This belief gives everyone a good reason to conform to R himself  Everyone believes others are doing the best they can to conform to R R is not the only possible regularity meeting (3) and (4) Each of the preceding factors are in principle common knowledge

Label: solitude behavior disposition
Description: Solitude is most often linked to low levels of loneliness and positive emotions when it is chosen solitude (due to enjoying solitude) and spent in active ways; people with a propensity to ruminate and those who choose solitude because they do not like being with others tend to suffer the most in solitude - Julie

Label: gerotranscendence disposition
Description: A gerotranscendence disposition that, if realized, is realized as a shift in perspective from a materialistic and rational worldview to a more cosmic and transcendent one, occurring in late life.

Label: social isolation behavior disposition
Description: Social isolation ranges from the voluntary withdrawal from social interaction, where an individual chooses to disengage for personal or situational reasons to involuntary isolation, where separation from others is imposed externally or occurs against th eindividual's will.

Label: feeling of loneliness
Description: Feeling of loneliness is a subjective and often distressing emotional state that arises from a perceived discrepancy between an individualâs desired and actual social relationships. It encompasses feelings of emptiness, abandonment, or disconnection, can develop over time, and is influenced by personal, social, and situational factors including identity, expectations, life events, and socio-economic conditions.

Label: theory information content entity
Description: An information content entity that represents a systematic and scientific framework for explaining, predicting, or understanding phenomena in a domain.

Label: psychological theory
Description: A theory information content entity that provides a systematic framework for explaining, predicting, or understanding psychological phenomena.

Label: social convoy model
Description: The convoy model of social relations describes how social relationships contribute to an individualâs health and well-being from a life course and lifespan perspective.

Label: ego integrity theory (Erikson)
Description: It states that personality develops across the lifespan through eight sequential psychosocial crises, where the successful resolution of each crisis fosters virtues (e.g., hope, fidelity, or, in later life, wisdom) and lays the foundation of success during the next crisis.

Label: self-transcendence theory
Description: A psychological theory that describes the developmental process by which individuals shift focus beyond self-concern toward broader universal, existential, or spiritual values, especially in later life.

Label: transcendence disposition
Description: A mental disposition that, if realized, is realized in shifting focus or perspective beyond the boundaries of personal self-concern toward broader universal or transcendent concerns.

Label: social isolation process
Description: A process in which an individual or group is separated from social contact, interaction, or community participation.

Label: need for a sense of belonging to a community
Description: A psychological need that, if unsatisfied, is realized in experiencing a desire to feel connected to and accepted by a social group or community.

Label: Dataset
Description: A set of RDF triples that are published, maintained or aggregated by a single provider.

Label: Subset
Description: An OBO subset, used for grouping ontology terms by category or application domain.

Label: Datatype
Description: The class of RDF datatypes.

Label: All Different
Description: The class of collections of pairwise different individuals.

Label: Axiom
Description: The class of annotated axioms for which the RDF serialization needs to be reified.

Label: Class
Description: The class of OWL classes.

Label: Named Individual
Description: The class of named individuals.

Label: Ontology
Description: The class of OWL ontologies.

Label: Restriction
Description: The class of property restrictions.

Label: has behavioural attribute
Description: A relation that links an individual human behaviour to a behavioural attribute.

Label: serves behavioural function
Description: Realises the human life function of an individual human behaviour.

Label: has behavioural outcome
Description: Causal relation between two entities in which a behaviour brings into existence, causes to occur, destroys, prevents from occurring, or changes an entity.

Label: is enacted by
Description: Has participant that relates a behaviour to the person enacting the behaviour.

Label: uses
Description: Has participant that relates a behaviour to a material entity that the person enacting the behaviour intends to enable or facilitate the behaviour.

Label: has behavioural companion
Description: Has participant that relates a behaviour to another sentient being that accompanies the person enacting the behaviour.

Label: has behavioural target
Description: Has participant that relates the behaviour to an object that the person enacting the behaviour intends to influence.

Label: is behavioural outcome of
Description: Inverse of has behavioural outcome relation.

Label: has end time
Description: A relation between an occurrent and a one-dimensional temporal entity that designates the latest boundary of the occurrent.

Label: has start time
Description: A relation between an occurrent and a one-dimensional temporal entity that designates the earliest boundary of the occurrent.

Label: datatype
Description: An XSD datatype used by typed literals in a dataset partition.

Label: datatypePartition
Description: A subset of a dataset that contains only triples whose objects are typed literals of a given datatype.

Label: language
Description: A language tag used by plain literals in a dataset partition.

Label: languagePartition
Description: A subset of a dataset that contains only triples whose objects are plain literals in a given language.

Label: objectClassPartition
Description: A subset of a dataset that contains only triples whose objects are instances of a given class.

Label: realises
Description: Paraphrase of elucidation: a relation between a process and a realizable entity, where there is some material entity that is bearer of the realizable entity and participates in the process, and the realizable entity comes to be realized in the course of the process

Label: occurs in
Description: Paraphrase of definition: a relation between a process and an independent continuant, in which the process takes place entirely within the independent continuant

Label: exists at
Description: A relation between a continuant or occurrent and a temporal region at which the entity exists.

Label: has occurrent part
Description: A mereological relation between two occurrents where one is a part of the other.

Label: occupies temporal region
Description: A relation between an occurrent and the temporal region it occupies.

Label: BFO OWL specification label
Description: Relates an entity in the ontology to the name of the variable used to represent it in the code that generates the BFO OWL file from the lispy specification.

Label: BFO CLIF specification label
Description: Relates an entity in the ontology to the term used to represent it in the CLIF specification of BFO2.

Label: editor preferred term
Description: The concise, meaningful, and human-friendly name for a class or property preferred by the ontology developer.

Label: example of usage
Description: A phrase describing how a term should be used and/or a citation to a work which uses it.

Label: has curation status
Description: An annotation property that links an ontology term to an individual that describes its curation status.

Label: definition
Description: The official definition, explaining the meaning of a class or property. Shall be Aristotelian, formal and concise.

Label: editor note
Description: An administrative note intended for the ontology editor; not typically included in the publication version of the ontology.

Label: term editor
Description: Name of editor entering the term in the file. The term editor is a point of contact for information regarding the term.

Label: alternative label
Description: Consider re-defing to: An alternative name for a class or property which can mean the same thing as the preferred name (semantically equivalent, narrow, broad or related).

Label: definition source
Description: Formal citation, e.g. identifier in external database to indicate or attribute source(s) for the definition.

Label: has obsolescence reason
Description: Relates an ontology term to the reason it was made obsolete.

Label: curator note
Description: An administrative note of use for a curator but of no use for a user.

Label: term tracker item
Description: The 'tracker item' can associate a tracker with a specific ontology term.

Label: ontology term requester
Description: The 'term requester' can credit the person, organization or project who request the ontology term.

Label: imported from
Description: For external terms/classes, the ontology from which the term was imported.

Label: expand assertion to
Description: A macro expansion tag applied to an annotation property which can be expanded into a more detailed axiom or set of axioms.

Label: OBO foundry unique label
Description: An alternative name for a class or property which is unique across the OBO Foundry.

Label: elucidation
Description: An informal description used in BFO for terms that are difficult to define formally, providing a partial characterization of the term.

Label: has associated axiom(nl)
Description: An annotation property that links an axiom to a natural language description of that axiom.

Label: has associated axiom(fol)
Description: An annotation property that links an axiom to a formal first-order logic description of that axiom.

Label: has axiom id
Description: An annotation property that provides a unique identifier label for an axiom in an ontology.

Label: synonym
Description: An annotation property used in the Mental Functioning Ontology of Emotions (MFOEM) to declare synonymous terms for ontology entities.

Label: defined by construct
Description: An annotation property that links an ontology term to the theoretical construct or framework by which it is defined.

Label: characteristic of
Description: Note that this relation was previously called "inheres in", but was changed to be called "characteristic of" because BFO2 uses "inheres in" in a more restricted fashion. This relation differs from BFO2:inheres_in in two respects: (1) it does not impose a range constraint, and thus it allows qualities of processes, as well as of information entities, whereas BFO2 restricts inheres_in to only apply to independent continuants (2) it is declared functional, i.e. something can only be a characteristic of one thing.

Label: has participant
Description: A relation between a process and a continuant, where the continuant is somehow involved in the process.

Label: temporal interpretation
Description: A relation that links an ontology term to the temporal interpretation assigned to it.

Label: happens during
Description: X happens_during Y iff: start(Y) is before or simultaneous with start(X), and end(X) is before or simultaneous with end(Y).

Label: temporally related to
Description: A relation that holds between two occurrents. This is a grouping relation that collects together all the Allen relations.

Label: causally related to
Description: A relation that holds between two entities where one is a cause of the other, either directly or indirectly.

Label: causal relation between entities
Description: A causal relation that holds between any two entities, including material entities, processes, and qualities.

Label: contributor
Description: An entity responsible for making contributions to the resource.

Label: contributor
Description: An entity responsible for making contributions to the resource.

Label: created
Description: Date of creation of the resource.

Label: description
Description: An account of the resource.

Label: license
Description: A legal document giving official permission to do something with the resource.

Label: modified
Description: Date on which the resource was changed.

Label: source
Description: A related resource from which the described resource is derived.

Label: title
Description: A name given to the resource.

Label: lastUpdatedOn
Description: The date this resource was most recently updated.

Label: version
Description: A string identifying the version of the resource.

Label: SDGIO 00010069
Description: A predicate from the Sustainable Development Goals Interface Ontology (SDGIO) used to link concepts to SDG-related indicators or goals.

Label: class
Description: The rdfs:Class that is the rdf:type or rdfs:subClassOf of subjects in a class partition.

Label: classPartition
Description: A subset of a void:Dataset that contains only the entities of a certain rdfs:Class.

Label: distinctObjects
Description: The total number of distinct objects in a void:Dataset.

Label: distinctSubjects
Description: The total number of distinct subjects in a void:Dataset.

Label: entities
Description: The total number of entities that are described in a void:Dataset.

Label: properties
Description: The total number of distinct properties in a void:Dataset.

Label: property
Description: The rdf:Property that is the predicate of all triples in a property partition.

Label: propertyPartition
Description: A subset of a void:Dataset that contains only the triples of a certain rdf:Property.

Label: triples
Description: The total number of triples contained in a void:Dataset.

Label: created_by
Description: Username of the person who created the ontology term.

Label: creation_date
Description: Date and time of the ontology term creation.

Label: hasAlternativeId
Description: An alternative identifier for the ontology term.

Label: hasDbXref
Description: A cross-reference to an external database entry.

Label: hasExactSynonym
Description: An exact synonym for the ontology term.

Label: hasOBONamespace
Description: The OBO namespace associated with the ontology term.

Label: hasRelatedSynonym
Description: A related synonym for the ontology term.

Label: id
Description: The unique identifier for the ontology term.

Label: inSubset
Description: Indicates that the term is a member of a defined subset.

Label: first
Description: The first item in an RDF list.

Label: rest
Description: The rest of an RDF list after the first item.

Label: comment
Description: A human-readable description of a resource.

Label: domain
Description: A domain of the subject property.

Label: isDefinedBy
Description: The defini­tion of the subject resource.

Label: label
Description: A human-readable name for the subject.

Label: range
Description: A range of the subject property.

Label: seeAlso
Description: Further information about the subject resource.

Label: subClassOf
Description: The subject is a subclass of a class.

Label: subPropertyOf
Description: The subject is a subproperty of a property.

Label: annotatedProperty
Description: The property that determines the predicate of an annotated axiom or annotated annotation.

Label: annotatedSource
Description: The property that determines the subject of an annotated axiom or annotated annotation.

Label: annotatedTarget
Description: The property that determines the object of an annotated axiom or annotated annotation.

Label: deprecated
Description: The annotation property that indicates that a given entity has been deprecated.

Label: disjointWith
Description: The property that determines that two given classes are disjoint.

Label: distinctMembers
Description: The property that determines the collection of pairwise different individuals in an owl:AllDifferent axiom.

Label: equivalentClass
Description: The property that determines that two given classes are equivalent.

Label: onProperty
Description: The property that determines the property that a property restriction refers to.

Label: oneOf
Description: The property that determines the collection of individuals or data values that build an enumeration.

Label: someValuesFrom
Description: The property that determines the class that an existential property restriction refers to.

Label: unionOf
Description: The property that determines the collection of classes or data ranges that build a union.

Label: versionIRI
Description: The property that identifies the version IRI of an ontology.

Label: versionInfo
Description: The annotation property that provides version information for an ontology or another OWL construct.

Label: altLabel
Description: An alternative lexical label for a resource.

Label: definition
Description: A statement or formal explanation of the meaning of a concept.

Label: prefLabel
Description: The preferred lexical label for a resource, in a given language.

Label: alternateName
Description: An alias for the item.

Label: derived from
Description: Indicates the word, morpheme, or phrase from which a lexeme, form or sense is derived.
